source: Learning to Match Using Local and Distributed Representations of Text for Web Search

concepts.finding: Combines local (exact term matching signals like TF-IDF, BM25) and distributed (embedding-based semantic matching) representations for ad-hoc retrieval. Two-layer neural network fuses both signal types. Local features capture precision, distributed features capture recall. Combined model outperforms either alone by 10-15% MAP.
concepts.implication: Keyword-intent matching benefits from both lexical (exact keyword match to intent labels) and semantic (embedding similarity) signals. Hybrid model: BM25-style lexical overlap between keyword and intent description + embedding similarity. Lexical for precision, semantic for recall.
concepts.open_question: What is the optimal weighting between lexical and semantic signals for keyword-intent matching? Does it vary by domain?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A hybrid keyword-intent matcher (lexical BM25 + semantic embedding similarity) outperforms either pure lexical or pure semantic matching for keyword-to-intent assignment F1.
